revision.text:
[[ja:長法寺南原古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun |image = |alternate_name = |part_of = {{ill|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qid=Q123117022|1=Otokuni Kofun Cluster}} |location = {{ill|Nagaokakyo|qid=Q861246|1=Nagaokakyō, Kyoto}} |coordinates = 34.931208 |coordinates = 135.675250 |omitted = JP-26 |type = [[Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] (Square-Front Rear-Square Mound) |map_size = Total length: 62 m
Height: 6.4 m (Rear-Square Section) |burial = Rear-Square: {{ill|Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|竪穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=竪穴式石室|4=zh|5=豎穴式石室|1=Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tateana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} ({{ill|Waritakegata Sarcophagus|ja|割竹形木棺|1=Waritakegata Mokkan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Sarcophagus|13=User:Immanuelle/Waritakegata Mokkan|qq=}} inside)
Front Section: Small pit-style stone chamber |burial goods = 4 [[Sankaku-en Shinjū-kyo|Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirrors]] and other grave goods; {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} |Accompanying Kofuns = |built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} |Burial Individual = |Mausoleum = |Historic Site = [[National Historic Site]] "Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun"
(Part of "Otokuni Kofun Cluster") |Historic Site = |notes = |map = Japan Kyoto city |type = Square-Front Rear-Square Mound - South |map_dot_label = Chōhōji
Minamibara Kofun |map_dot_label = Right }} '''Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun''' (長法寺南原古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Chōhōji Minamibara, [[Nagaokakyō City]], {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The mound is part of the [[Otokuni Kofun Cluster]] and has been designation1 as a [[National Historic Site of Japan|National Historic Site]] (as part of the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster"). == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+National Historic Site "Otokuni Kofun Cluster" (13 Tumuli) !Phase !Kofun name !type !area !burial |- | rowspan="2" |1 |{{ill|Itsukahara Kofun|ja|五塚原古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukahara Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} |Mound length: 91 m | |- |{{ill|Motoinari Kofun|ja|元稲荷古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Motoinari Kofun|qq=}} |{{ill|Zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |Mound length: 94 m |Vertical chamber tomb |- |2 |{{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|qid=Q11457885|lt=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|1=Terado Ōtsuka Kofun}} |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 98 m |2 vertical chamber tombs |- |3 |'''Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun''' |Zenpō-kōhō-fun |Mound length: 60 m |Vertical chamber tomb Small Sarcophagus chamber |- | rowspan="2" |4 |{{ill|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|en|Tennō-no-Mori Kofun|qid=Q28608917|lt=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun|1=Tenno-no-Mori Kofun}} |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 83 m | |- |{{ill|Toriimae Kofun|ja|鳥居前古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toriimae Kofun|qq=}} |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 51 m |Vertical chamber tomb |- |5 |{{ill|Igenoyama Kofun|en|Igenoyama Kofun|qid=Q11492427|lt=Igenoyama Kofun|1=Igenoyama Kofun}} |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 128 m |Vertical chamber tomb? Grave goods storage facility |- |7 |Minami-jō Kofun |{{ill|Enpun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enpun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |Diameter: 23.5 m | |- | rowspan="4" |9 |Shiba Kofun |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 32.7 m | |- |Inouchi-Kurumazuka Kofun |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 37 m | |- |{{ill|Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|物集女車塚古墳|1=Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monoshirume Kurumazuka Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozume Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 48 m |Horizontal chamber tomb |- |{{ill|Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|ja|井ノ内稲荷塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inouchi Inarizuka Kofun|qq=}} |Zenpō-kōen-fun |Mound length: 46 m |Horizontal chamber tomb Direct wooden coffin burial |- |11 |Imazato Ōtsuka Kofun |Enpun |Diameter: 45 m |Horizontal chamber tomb |}
The Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun is situated at the highest point (145 meters above sea level) of a ridge extending from the Nishiyama Mountains in the southwestern Kyoto Basin. Now overgrown with bamboo thickets, the mound has undergone significant modification over time. The burial facilities were first discovered and surveyed in {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}} and further excavations took place from {{ill|1981|en|1981|qid=Q2437|lt=1981|1=1981}} to {{ill|1989|en|1989|qid=Q2425|lt=1989|1=1989}}. The mound's type is a [[zenpō-kōhō-fun]] with the front section facing south. The rear-square section was built in three tiers, while the front section comprises two tiers. The lower portion of the mound was carved out of natural ground, while the u11er portion was constructed with piled soil. The surface lacks stone coverings (''fukiishi'') but has yielded cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}, including ''asa-gao''-shaped examples. The mound's perimeter features a terrace (2 m wide and 0.4 m high) and a surrounding moat approximately 1 m wide. Two burial facilities were constructed: a [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|pit-type stone chamber]] at the rear-square apex and a smaller [[Vertical Pit-Style Stone Chamber|pit-style stone chamber]] at the front apex. The rear chamber contained a split-bamboo wooden coffin and yielded numerous burial goods, including 6 bronze mirrors (4 [[Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|sankaku-en shinjū-kyo]]), jade items, iron weapons, farming tools, and stone tools. Among these, one triangular-rimmed divine beast mirror is significant as it matches mirrors found at 9 other sites nationwide, providing evidence of [[Yamato Court]]'s distribution of prestige goods. The mound is dated to the late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}}, an important time in archaeological studies due to the presence of multiple triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors. It stands out among early mounds in the region due to its distinctive type, lack of stone coverings, and dual burial facilities. The site was designation1 a National Historic Site in {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}} as part of the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster." To the east of the mound lies the Minamibara East Kofun Cluster, which contains tombs with haniwa coffins. == Historic Excavations == * {{ill|1934|en|1934|qid=Q18714|lt=1934|1=1934}}: Discovery and investigation of the pit-type stone chamber during bamboo clearing (1st investigation by [[Sueji Umehara]] and Kyoto Prefecture; reported in {{ill|1937|en|1937|qid=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}}). * [[1981–1989]]: Excavation campaigns (2nd–6th investigations by [[:en:Osaka University|Osaka University]] and Nagaokakyō City). == Kofun Details == Mound area: * Total length: 62 m * Rear-Square Section: ''' Side length: 44 m ''' Height: ~6.4 m ''' Three-tiered construction * Front Section: ''' Length: 18 m ''' Width: 28 m ''' Height: ~4 m ''' Two-tiered construction == burial goods == burial goods from the rear-square chamber include: * 6 bronze mirrors: ''' Triangular-rimmed mirrors with god-beast motifs and inscriptions ''' Long-lasting inscription mirrors * Jade items: ''' 1 ''magatama'' ''' 19 tubular beads ''' 287 small glass beads * Bronze weapons: ''' 3 bronze arrowheads * Iron weapons: ''' 1 iron sword ''' 7 short swords ''' 123 iron arrowheads * Iron agricultural tools: ''' 1 chisel ''' 12 axes ''' 5 spades ''' 5 small knives * Stone tools: ''' 2 stone grinders ''' 1 stone pestle == Historic Site == === National Historic Site === * Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun (part of the "Otokuni Kofun Cluster") - designation1 February 13, {{ill|2018|en|2018|qid=Q25291|lt=2018|1=2018}}. == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|en|Tokyo National Museum|qid=Q653433|lt=Tokyo National Museum|1=Tokyo National Museum}} (Tokyo): Houses burial goods from the Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun. * Nagaokakyō Municipal Archaeological Research Center (Nagaokakyō City): Exhibits haniwa from the site. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * {{National Historic Site Database|401|1638|Otokuni Kofun Cluster|qq=}} {{Kofun navbar|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|長法寺南原古墳|version=101614263|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Chōhōji Minamibara Kofun}} [[ja:長法寺南原古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q123116949}} [[Category:乙訓古墳群]] [[Category:National historic sites in Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Kyoto prefecture]] [[Category:Zenpō-kōhō-fun]] [[Category:長岡京市の歴史]]
